title: Port to MacOS
body:
* Refactor Tunnel to support selecting port for remote sshd
* Refactor ssh tunnel to support MacOS version of ssh
* Refactor Tunnel.close() to find and kill off zombie siblings
* Add psutil dependency
* Add logging setup, letting library produce debugging records
* Clean up Tunnel API
* Fix test_runner.sh to propagate returncode to caller

comments.body:
@jwhonce Looking at the failing tests more.

It looks like a call to `inotify_init1` is failing with `EMFILE`. This could be max open files for the process, but it could also be maximum inotify instances for a user being reached. The limit (/proc/sys/fs/inotify/max_user_instances) is per-UID, and set to 128 on my system. Might be more likely than exhausting the default 1024 file descriptors?

@mheon Below are the crazy numbers I tried last night just for debugging. (I reordered the output so it made more sense.)  I didn't tweak fs.inotify.max_user_instances but I will and let you know the results.
 
+ ulimit -n 999999
+ ulimit -n
999999
+ sysctl fs.inotify.max_user_watches=999999
fs.inotify.max_user_watches = 999999
+ setenforce 0
setenforce: SELinux is disabled
+ prlimit --nofile=999999:999999 podman --storage-driver=vfs --root=/tmp/podman.c3ilOF6z0B/crio --runroot=/tmp/podman.c3ilOF6z0B/crio-run --cni-config-dir=/tmp/podman.c3ilOF6z0B/cni/net.d varlink --timeout=0 unix:/tmp/podman.c3ilOF6z0B/podman/io.projectatomic.podman
+ prlimit --pid 8864 --nofile
RESOURCE DESCRIPTION                SOFT   HARD UNITS
NOFILE   max number of open files 999999 999999 files
+ lsof
+ wc -l
787
+ sysctl fs.file-nr
fs.file-nr = 1024	0	788581
+ sysctl fs.file-max
fs.file-max = 788581
@lsm5 FYI -- There are dependency updates in the spec files.
